pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates alert with 'TARGET: Algol (Beta Persei, HD 19356)'
setup_task.sh specifies RA 03h 08m 10.13s (3.1361 hours) and verifier.py sets TARGET_RA = 3.1361
setup_task.sh specifies Dec +40d 57m 20.3s (40.9556 degrees) and verifier.py sets TARGET_DEC = 40.9556
setup_task.sh configures slot 2 as V-band and the alert requires V-band
setup_task.sh alert specifies 'Exposure time: 10 seconds per frame'
setup_task.sh alert specifies 'Minimum frames: 20' and verifier.py checks min_fits = 20
setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/Images/eb_timing and alert requires upload to /home/ga/Images/eb_timing/algol/
setup_task.sh alert specifies to submit O-C timing report to this path, and verifier.py checks it
setup_task.sh dynamically creates the alert file at this path
Matches task scope and verifier.py comment 'Occupation: Astronomer / AAVSO Eclipsing Binary Section Observer'
Matches the astronomical observation and O-C reporting nature of the task

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a synthetically generated 'AAVSO ECLIPSING BINARY SECTION — TIMING ALERT' document created during setup. The true expected observation coordinates are RA 3.1361 hours and Dec 40.9556 degrees, derived directly from the alert document and matched by the verifier's 30 arcminute tolerance. The task explicitly requires capturing at least 20 FITS images using the V-band filter (slot 2) with a 10-second exposure in the /home/ga/Images/eb_timing/algol directory. An O-C timing report must be produced at /home/ga/Documents/algol_oc_report.txt containing specific keywords like 'Algol', the JD, V-band filter, CCD method, and at least 20 observations.
